effect: Bearing seizure, unplanned shutdown, potential rotor damage
cause: Lubricant degradation due to extended high-load operation exceeding 72 hours
controls: Real-time temperature trend monitoring; Kalman residual > 3°C for 10 consecutive minutes
recommended_action: Check lubricant level and oil quality. Schedule bearing inspection within 24 hours. Reduce load to 80% if temperature continues rising.

effect: Loss of separation efficiency, product contamination, potential mechanical damage to packing
cause: Excessive vapor load causing liquid entrainment in upper stages
controls: ΔP trending; reflux ratio monitoring; flow/ΔP correlation check
recommended_action: Reduce feed flow by 10%. Increase reflux ratio by 5%. Monitor ΔP trend for 30 minutes. If ΔP does not decrease, initiate controlled shutdown.

effect: Reduced isotope enrichment, increased energy consumption, potential ice formation on column shell
cause: Insulation degradation at feed stage due to thermal cycling fatigue
controls: External shell temperature survey (monthly IR thermography); cascade temperature profile deviation check
recommended_action: Schedule IR thermography inspection. Compare cascade temperature profile against baseline. Repair insulation at identified leak points during next maintenance window.

effect: Reduced heat transfer efficiency, increased pressure drop, higher utility costs
cause: Gradual deposit buildup on tube side due to trace impurities in process stream
controls: UA (overall heat transfer coefficient) trending; ΔT approach temperature monitoring
recommended_action: Monitor UA trend weekly. If UA drops below 80% of design, schedule chemical cleaning during next planned shutdown.

effect: Overheating of tube bundle, potential tube rupture, loss of heating capacity
cause: Insufficient liquid level due to downstream valve FV-303 malfunction or level controller LT-301 drift
controls: Level transmitter cross-validation; reboiler outlet temperature vs. steam flow correlation
recommended_action: Verify FV-303 position feedback. Check LT-301 calibration. Inspect reboiler level sight glass. Do NOT increase steam flow until liquid level is confirmed.

effect: Oscillating flow control, poor product quality, increased actuator wear
cause: Control valve packing friction due to aging graphite packing material
controls: Valve positioner feedback vs. commanded position deviation; flow oscillation amplitude monitoring
recommended_action: Check valve positioner feedback signal for hysteresis. Perform partial stroke test. Replace valve packing during next maintenance window if deviation > 5%.

effect: Incorrect feed-forward control, suboptimal reflux ratio, gradual yield loss
cause: Orifice plate erosion or DP cell zero-point drift over extended service period
controls: Monthly mass balance reconciliation; cross-validation with redundant flow measurement FT-302
recommended_action: Cross-validate FT-301 against FT-302 and tank level drop rate. If deviation > 3%, schedule calibration. Replace orifice plate if erosion exceeds 10% of original thickness.

effect: Condensate pump damage, fluctuating condenser pressure, potential loss of vacuum
cause: Insufficient NPSH (Net Positive Suction Head) due to high condensate temperature or low level in hotwell
controls: Condenser pressure stability monitoring; pump vibration spectrum analysis; NPSH margin calculation
recommended_action: Check condensate pump suction pressure. Verify hotwell level control. If NPSH margin < 1 meter, reduce condensate temperature by increasing cooling water flow.
